passage.title: Door Basket Notes
passage.text: Our group keeps a routine strip beside the toy shelf. After school, Joon looks at it before dinner. The routine strip has short notes for homework, piano, and reading for the day's job. Dad asks us to finish our routine in order. Sometimes we compare today's note with yesterday's note and notice one small change. We like the routine strip because it helps us clean up faster.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the routine strip is used.
The first sentence says the routine strip is beside the toy shelf.

questions.prompt: When does Joon use the routine strip?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Joon uses it after school.

questions.prompt: What does the routine strip have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the routine strip has short notes.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
